name: Margarita
instructions: Rub the rim of a coupe glass with lime and dip in salt. Combine tequila, orange liqueur, and lime juice in a shaker with ice. Shake vigorously for 15 seconds and strain into the prepared glass. Cointreau is the classic orange liqueur choice. Many bartenders use equal parts orange liqueur and lime (2:1:1) — try it both ways and decide your preference.

name: Moscow Mule
instructions: Fill a copper mug with ice. Add vodka and lime juice, then top with ginger beer. Stir gently to combine.

name: Negroni
instructions: Combine all ingredients in a mixing glass with ice. Stir for 30 seconds. Strain into a rocks glass over a large ice cube. Express orange peel over top. Many bartenders prefer 1.5oz gin for a spirit-forward version — try it both ways.

name: Whiskey Sour
instructions: Dry shake all ingredients without ice for 15 seconds. Add ice and shake again vigorously. Double-strain into a rocks glass over ice.

name: Gin & Tonic
instructions: Fill a highball glass with ice. Pour gin over ice, then pour tonic water slowly down the side to preserve carbonation. Stir once gently.

name: Old Fashioned
instructions: In a rocks glass, add syrup and both bitters. Add bourbon and a large ice cube. Stir gently for 20–30 seconds. Express an orange peel over the top.

name: Aperol Spritz
instructions: Fill a large wine glass with ice. Pour prosecco first, then Aperol, then a splash of soda water. Stir gently once.

name: Mojito
instructions: Lightly muddle mint leaves with lime juice and simple syrup. Add rum and fill with crushed ice. Top with soda water and stir gently.

name: Cosmopolitan
instructions: Shake all ingredients with ice until very cold. Strain into a chilled martini glass.

name: Paloma
instructions: Salt-rim a highball glass. Fill with ice. Add tequila, grapefruit juice, lime juice, and syrup. Top with soda and stir gently.

name: Dark & Stormy
instructions: Fill a highball glass with ice. Add lime juice and ginger beer, then float the dark rum on top by pouring over the back of a spoon.

name: Tom Collins
instructions: Shake gin, lemon juice, and simple syrup with ice. Strain into a Collins glass filled with ice. Top with soda water and stir briefly.

name: French 75
instructions: Shake gin, lemon juice, and syrup with ice. Strain into a champagne flute and top with Champagne.

name: Last Word
instructions: Shake all ingredients vigorously with ice. Double-strain into a chilled coupe.

name: Aviation
instructions: Combine all ingredients in a shaker with ice. Shake and double-strain into a chilled coupe.

name: Gimlet
instructions: Shake all ingredients with ice. Strain into a chilled coupe or a rocks glass over ice.

name: Vesper Martini
instructions: Combine all ingredients in a shaker with ice. Shake or stir — your choice. Strain into a chilled martini glass.

name: Bee's Knees
instructions: Make honey syrup by mixing equal parts honey and warm water. Shake all ingredients with ice and strain into a chilled coupe.

name: Clover Club
instructions: Dry shake all ingredients to emulsify the egg white. Add ice and shake again. Double-strain into a chilled coupe.

name: Manhattan
instructions: Combine all ingredients in a mixing glass with ice. Stir for 30 seconds. Strain into a chilled coupe or martini glass.

name: Rob Roy
instructions: Stir all ingredients with ice in a mixing glass. Strain into a chilled coupe.

name: Boulevardier
instructions: Stir all ingredients with ice. Strain into a rocks glass over a large cube.

name: Paper Plane
instructions: Shake all equal-parts ingredients with ice. Double-strain into a chilled coupe.

name: Penicillin
instructions: Shake blended Scotch, lemon, honey syrup, and ginger syrup with ice. Strain into a rocks glass over ice. Float the peaty Islay Scotch on top.

name: Gold Rush
instructions: Shake all ingredients with ice. Strain into a rocks glass over a large ice cube.

name: New York Sour
instructions: Dry shake bourbon, lemon, syrup, and egg white. Add ice, shake again. Strain into a rocks glass over ice. Float red wine over the back of a spoon.

name: Blood & Sand
instructions: Shake all ingredients with ice. Double-strain into a chilled coupe Cherry Heering is the traditional cherry liqueur.

name: Vieux Carré
instructions: Stir all ingredients with ice in a mixing glass. Strain into a rocks glass over ice.

name: Amaretto Sour
instructions: Dry shake all ingredients. Add ice, shake again vigorously. Double-strain into a rocks glass over ice.

name: Vodka Martini
instructions: Stir vodka and vermouth with ice in a mixing glass for 30 seconds. Strain into a chilled martini glass.

name: Espresso Martini
instructions: Shake all ingredients very vigorously with ice. Double-strain into a chilled martini glass. The foam comes from the espresso crema.

name: White Russian
instructions: Fill a rocks glass with ice. Add vodka and Coffee Liqueur. Float cream on top — pour slowly over the back of a spoon.

name: Sex on the Beach
instructions: Fill a highball glass with ice. Add vodka and schnapps. Pour OJ and cranberry side by side for a layered look, then stir Peach Schnapps is the classic peach liqueur here.

name: Harvey Wallbanger
instructions: Fill a highball glass with ice. Add vodka and OJ, stir. Float Galliano on top by pouring over the back of a spoon.

name: Porn Star Martini
instructions: Shake all ingredients except Prosecco with ice. Double-strain into a martini glass. Serve with a shot of Prosecco on the side.

name: Piña Colada
instructions: Blend all ingredients with 1 cup of ice until smooth. Pour into a chilled hurricane glass.

name: Mai Tai
instructions: Shake all ingredients except dark rum with ice. Strain over crushed ice. Float dark rum over the top.

name: Jungle Bird
instructions: Shake all ingredients with ice. Strain over ice into a rocks glass or tiki mug.

name: Zombie
instructions: Shake all ingredients with crushed ice. Pour into a tall glass. Limit: 2 per customer (per Donn Beach's original rule!).

name: Hemingway Daiquiri
instructions: Shake all ingredients hard with ice. Double-strain into a chilled coupe.

name: Cuba Libre
instructions: Squeeze lime into a highball glass filled with ice. Add rum and top with cola. Stir briefly.

name: Tommy's Margarita
instructions: Shake all ingredients with ice. Strain into a rocks glass over ice. The pure agave-sweetened version — no orange liqueur.

name: Spicy Margarita
instructions: Muddle jalapeño slices in the shaker. Add tequila, triple sec, and lime juice with ice. Shake and double-strain into a salt-rimmed glass over ice Cointreau is the classic orange liqueur choice.

name: Tequila Sunrise
instructions: Fill a highball glass with ice. Add tequila and orange juice. Pour grenadine slowly down the inside of the glass — it sinks and creates the sunrise gradient. Do not stir.

name: Oaxacan Old Fashioned
instructions: Combine all ingredients in a rocks glass over a large ice cube. Stir for 20 seconds until chilled.

name: Sidecar
instructions: Shake all ingredients with ice. Strain into a chilled coupe with a sugared rim.

name: Brandy Alexander
instructions: Shake all ingredients with ice until very cold. Double-strain into a chilled coupe.

name: Stinger
instructions: Shake or stir all ingredients with ice. Strain into a chilled coupe or over crushed ice.

name: Kir Royale
instructions: Pour crème de cassis into a chilled flute. Top with Champagne — do not stir.

name: Bellini
instructions: Pour peach purée into a chilled flute. Top with Prosecco and stir very gently.

name: Mimosa
instructions: Pour orange juice into a flute first, then top with Champagne. Stir very gently.

name: Spritz Veneziano
instructions: Fill a wine glass with ice. Add Campari, top with Prosecco then soda. Stir once gently.

name: Americano
instructions: Build in a rocks glass over ice. Add Campari and vermouth, top with soda and stir briefly.

name: Naked & Famous
instructions: Shake all equal-parts ingredients with ice. Double-strain into a chilled coupe.

name: Bramble
instructions: Shake gin, lemon juice, and syrup with ice. Strain into a rocks glass over crushed ice. Drizzle crème de mûre over the top — it bleeds through the ice.

name: Cynar Sour
instructions: Dry shake all ingredients, then shake with ice. Double-strain into a rocks glass over ice.

name: Shirley Temple
instructions: Fill a highball glass with ice. Add grenadine and orange juice. Top with ginger ale and stir briefly.

name: Virgin Mojito
instructions: Lightly muddle mint with lime juice and syrup. Fill glass with crushed ice. Top with soda water and stir gently.

name: Arnold Palmer
instructions: Fill a tall glass with ice. Add iced tea and lemonade in equal parts. Stir gently.

name: Sazerac
instructions: Rinse a chilled rocks glass with absinthe and discard. In a mixing glass, stir remaining ingredients with ice. Strain into the prepared glass.

name: Pisco Sour
instructions: Combine pisco, lime juice, syrup, and egg white in a shaker. Dry shake first, then add ice and shake again. Strain into a coupe. Add bitters drops on the foam.

name: Mint Julep
instructions: Gently muddle mint with simple syrup in a julep cup. Pack tightly with crushed ice. Add bourbon and stir briefly. Top with more crushed ice and garnish with a mint bouquet.

name: Hanky Panky
instructions: Stir all ingredients with ice until well chilled. Strain into a chilled coupe and express an orange twist over the top.

name: Corpse Reviver #2
instructions: Rinse chilled coupe with absinthe and discard. Shake gin, lemon, orange liqueur (Cointreau is traditional), and Lillet Blanc with ice. Strain into the rinsed glass. Strain into the rinsed glass.

name: Bijou
instructions: Stir gin, sweet vermouth, and green Chartreuse with ice. Strain into a chilled coupe. Garnish.

name: Between the Sheets
instructions: Shake all ingredients with ice and double-strain into a chilled coupe Grand Marnier or Cointreau are classic as the orange liqueur.

name: 20th Century
instructions: Shake all ingredients with ice. Double-strain into a chilled coupe Cointreau is a good choice for the orange liqueur.

name: Tuxedo
instructions: Stir gin, dry vermouth, maraschino, and absinthe with ice. Strain into a chilled coupe.

name: Toronto
instructions: Stir rye, Fernet, syrup, and bitters with ice. Strain into a chilled coupe and garnish with orange twist Fernet-Branca is the standard choice for fernet.

name: Whiskey Smash
instructions: Muddle lemon wedges and mint with syrup in a shaker. Add bourbon and ice, shake well. Double-strain over fresh ice.

name: Brown Derby
instructions: Shake bourbon, grapefruit juice, and honey syrup with ice. Double-strain into a chilled coupe.

name: Fancy Free
instructions: Stir bourbon, maraschino, and bitters over ice. Strain into a rocks glass over a large ice cube.

name: Scotch Sour
instructions: Shake all ingredients with ice. Strain into a rocks glass over ice. Optional: float a dash of red wine.

name: Revolver
instructions: Stir bourbon, coffee liqueur, and bitters with ice. Strain into a chilled coupe. Flame orange twist over the top.

name: Suburban
instructions: Stir rye, dark rum, port, and bitters with ice. Strain into a chilled coupe.

name: Ramos Gin Fizz
instructions: Combine gin, citrus, syrup, cream, egg white, and orange flower water. Dry shake for 2 minutes. Add ice, shake again for 2 more minutes. Strain into a highball and top slowly with soda.

name: Singapore Sling
instructions: Shake gin, lemon, grenadine, Cointreau, pineapple, Bénédictine and cherry Heering with ice. Strain into a Collins glass with ice and top with soda Cherry Heering is the classic cherry liqueur; Cointreau or Triple Sec for the orange liqueur.

name: Southside
instructions: Shake gin, lime, syrup, and mint with ice. Double-strain into a chilled coupe.

name: Martinez
instructions: Stir gin, sweet vermouth, maraschino, and bitters with ice. Strain into a chilled coupe.

name: Gin Buck
instructions: Build in a highball glass over ice. Add gin and lemon juice, top with ginger ale. Stir briefly.

name: White Lady
instructions: Shake gin, Cointreau, lemon juice, and egg white with ice. Double-strain into a chilled coupe Cointreau is traditional as the orange liqueur.

name: Painkiller
instructions: Shake rum, juices, and coconut cream with ice. Strain over ice. Grate fresh nutmeg generously on top.

name: Three Dots and a Dash
instructions: Shake all ingredients with ice. Strain into a highball or tiki mug filled with crushed ice.

name: El Presidente
instructions: Stir rum, dry vermouth, curaçao, and grenadine with ice. Strain into a chilled coupe.

name: Mary Pickford
instructions: Shake rum, pineapple juice, grenadine, and maraschino with ice. Strain into a chilled coupe.

name: Rum Punch
instructions: Build over ice in a highball. Stir gently. Garnish. Follow the rule: one sour, two sweet, three strong, four weak Triple Sec or Cointreau for the orange liqueur.

name: Oaxacan Negroni
instructions: Stir mezcal, sweet vermouth, and Campari with ice. Strain over a large ice cube. Flame an orange twist over the top.

name: Tequila Old Fashioned
instructions: Stir tequila, agave, and bitters with ice. Strain over a large ice cube. Express orange twist over the top.

name: El Diablo
instructions: Build in a highball glass over ice. Add tequila, crème de cassis, and lime juice. Top with ginger beer.

name: Ranch Water
instructions: Build in a highball glass over ice. Add tequila and lime juice. Top with Topo Chico or sparkling mineral water.

name: Mezcal Sour
instructions: Shake all ingredients with ice. Strain over fresh ice into a chili-salt rimmed rocks glass.

name: Gimlet (Vodka)
instructions: Shake vodka, lime juice, and simple syrup with ice. Double-strain into a chilled coupe.

name: Vesper (Original)
instructions: Shake (Bond's way) or stir with ice. Strain into a chilled martini glass. Garnish with large lemon twist.

name: Vodka Collins
instructions: Shake vodka, lemon, and syrup with ice. Strain into a Collins glass with ice. Top with soda water.

name: Dirty Martini
instructions: Stir gin, dry vermouth, and olive brine in a mixing glass with ice for about 30 seconds. Strain into a chilled martini glass. Garnish with 2–3 olives on a pick. Use a quality brine — the jar your olives came in works well. The olive brine softens the gin's botanicals and adds a savory, briny depth. For a vodka dirty martini, substitute vodka for gin — equally classic.

name: Lemon Drop
instructions: Shake vodka, triple sec, and lemon juice with ice. Strain into a sugar-rimmed chilled martini glass.

name: Black Russian
instructions: Build in a rocks glass over ice. Add vodka then coffee liqueur. Stir gently.

name: Pisco Punch
instructions: Shake pisco, pineapple juice, lemon, and simple syrup with ice. Strain into a coupe or punch cup Cointreau works well as the orange liqueur.

name: Sangria
instructions: Combine wine, brandy, orange juice, triple sec, and syrup. Add fruit. Refrigerate for at least 2 hours. Serve over ice.

name: Mulled Wine
instructions: Gently heat red wine with brandy, cinnamon, cloves, star anise, orange peel, and sugar. Do not boil. Strain and serve warm.

name: Kalimotxo
instructions: Build in a highball over ice. Add equal parts red wine and cola. Stir gently. Add lime.

name: Garibaldi
instructions: Juice fresh oranges until fluffy (use a centrifugal juicer if possible). Build Campari over ice, top with fluffy OJ. Stir gently.

name: Negroni Sbagliato
instructions: Build over ice in a wine glass. Add Campari and sweet vermouth, top with Prosecco. Stir once and garnish.

name: Bamboo
instructions: Combine dry sherry, dry vermouth, and both bitters in a mixing glass with ice. Stir for 30 seconds until well chilled and diluted. Strain into a chilled coupe. Garnish with a lemon twist. The Bamboo is a low-ABV stirred classic — dry sherry as the base gives it a nutty, oxidative character. Note: if you substitute sweet vermouth for the dry, you have an Adonis — an equally excellent but very different drink.

name: Sherry Cobbler
instructions: Shake sherry, simple syrup, and orange juice with ice. Strain into a wine glass filled with crushed ice. Garnish elaborately.

name: Cynar Spritz
instructions: Build over ice in a wine glass. Add Cynar, top with Prosecco and a splash of soda. Stir gently.

name: B-52
instructions: Layer carefully in a shot glass: Coffee Liqueur first, then Baileys, then Grand Marnier. Pour each over the back of a spoon.

name: Jager Bomb
instructions: Fill a pint glass halfway with energy drink. Drop the shot glass of Jägermeister in. Drink immediately.

name: Tequila Shot
instructions: Lick the back of your hand, pour salt on it. Lick salt, shoot tequila, bite lime.

name: Kamikaze
instructions: Shake vodka, triple sec, and lime with ice. Strain into a shot glass or small martini glass Cointreau or Triple Sec for the orange liqueur.

name: Irish Coffee
instructions: Pre-heat a glass with hot water, discard. Add hot coffee, brown sugar, and Irish whiskey. Float lightly whipped cream on top. Do not stir.

name: Hot Toddy
instructions: Add whiskey, honey, lemon juice to a warm mug. Top with hot water. Stir and garnish.

name: Hot Buttered Rum
instructions: Mix butter batter (butter, brown sugar, cinnamon, nutmeg, cloves) with a spoon of hot water. Add rum and top with hot water.

name: Tom and Jerry
instructions: Beat egg yolk with sugar and rum; fold in stiff egg white. Add a spoonful of batter to a warm mug. Top with hot milk and more rum. Dust with nutmeg.

name: Aperol Tonic
instructions: Build over ice in a wine glass. Add Aperol and top with tonic water. Stir gently.

name: Spritz Bianco
instructions: Build over ice in a white wine glass. Add white wine and elderflower liqueur, top with soda. Stir once.

name: Lillet Spritz
instructions: Build in a wine glass over ice. Add Lillet Blanc, top with tonic water. Garnish with grapefruit twist.

name: Vermouth & Tonic
instructions: Build in a stemmed glass over ice. Pour dry vermouth, top with tonic water. Garnish.

name: Seedlip Sour
instructions: Shake all ingredients vigorously with ice. Double-strain into a chilled coupe.

name: Pineapple Cooler
instructions: Shake pineapple juice, lime, and coconut water with ice. Strain over ice and top with soda. Garnish.

name: Watermelon Agua Fresca
instructions: Blend watermelon with lime juice and simple syrup. Strain. Serve over ice with a splash of soda.

name: Ginger Lemonade
instructions: Combine lemon juice, ginger syrup, and water. Shake with ice. Strain over ice in a highball.

name: Sea Legs
garnish: Lime wheel or Angostura bitters line
instructions: Shake peated whisky, mezcal, orgeat, and lime juice with ice. Double-strain into a rocks glass over a single large ice cube (optional). Dash celery bitters over the top, then draw a line of Angostura if you have the skills — or float a lime wheel.

name: Enzoni
instructions: Add green grapes to a shaker and muddle gently. Add gin, Campari, lemon juice, and simple syrup. Shake with ice. Double-strain over fresh ice into a rocks glass. Garnish with a grape and orange slice. Created by Vincenzo Errico at Milk & Honey, NYC — a cross between a Negroni and a Gin Sour.

name: Division Bell
instructions: Combine all ingredients in a shaker with ice. Shake until well chilled. Double-strain into a chilled coupe. Express a grapefruit peel over the surface to release its oils, then discard. Created by Phil Ward at Mayahuel, NYC in 2009 — a mezcal riff on the Last Word, named after the Pink Floyd album he listened to while building the bar.

name: Daiquiri
instructions: Combine rum, lime juice, and simple syrup in a shaker with ice. Shake hard for 10–12 seconds. Double-strain into a chilled coupe. The Daiquiri is one of the six basic drinks in "The Fine Art of Mixing Drinks" — simplicity is its virtue.

name: Bloody Mary
instructions: Build in a highball glass over ice. Add vodka, tomato juice, lemon juice, Worcestershire, hot sauce, horseradish, and seasonings. Roll between two glasses to mix gently. Adjust seasoning to taste. Garnish generously.

name: Old Pal
instructions: Combine all ingredients in a mixing glass with ice. Stir for 30 seconds until well chilled. Strain into a chilled coupe. Express a lemon peel over the surface and discard or twist on the rim. A drier, boozier cousin of the Boulevardier.

name: Trinidad Sour
instructions: Combine all ingredients in a shaker with ice. Shake vigorously — the orgeat needs to integrate fully with the bitters. Double-strain into a chilled coupe. Created by Giuseppe González at Painkiller, NYC. An astonishing drink built on an entire ounce of Angostura bitters.

name: Bitter Giuseppe
instructions: Combine all ingredients in a mixing glass with ice. Stir for 30–40 seconds until well chilled and diluted. Strain over a large ice cube in a rocks glass. Express a lemon peel over the surface. Created by Stephen Cole. The equal-parts Cynar build makes this one of the most approachable amaro drinks.

name: Ferrari
instructions: Build over ice in a rocks glass or combine in a mixing glass and stir. Pour Fernet-Branca and Campari in equal parts over ice. Stir briefly. Express an orange peel over the surface. A bartender's handshake — two of the most polarising amari united Fernet-Branca is the standard fernet used here.

name: Trident
instructions: Combine all ingredients in a mixing glass with ice. Stir for 30 seconds. Strain into a chilled coupe. Express a lemon peel over the surface. Created by Robert Hess. An aquavit-based sibling of the Negroni using three low-ABV, underappreciated spirits.

name: Black Manhattan
instructions: Combine rye or bourbon with Averna in a mixing glass with ice. Add bitters. Stir for 30 seconds. Strain into a chilled coupe or Nick & Nora. Garnish with a cherry. Swap sweet vermouth for Averna amaro — darker, more herbal, slightly bitter Amaro Averna is the classic choice — rich, bittersweet, and herbal.

name: Benton's Old Fashioned
instructions: Fat-wash the bourbon: combine 1.5 oz warm bacon fat with bourbon, let sit 4 hours, freeze, and remove solidified fat. Combine fat-washed bourbon with maple syrup and bitters in a mixing glass with ice. Stir for 30 seconds. Strain over large ice. Created by Don Lee at PDT, NYC — the drink that launched the fat-washing technique.

name: Jet Pilot
instructions: Combine all ingredients in a blender with 1 cup of cracked ice. Blend for 5 seconds. Pour unstrained into a tiki mug. Garnish with a mint sprig and lime wheel. A vintage Don the Beachcomber original — high-octane rum blend with Donn's Spices.

name: Pearl Diver
instructions: Combine all ingredients with 1 cup of crushed ice in a blender. Blend briefly — about 5 seconds. Pour into a tiki mug or pearl diver glass. Gardenia Mix: blend 1 oz softened butter, 1 oz honey, 1 oz cinnamon syrup. A Don the Beachcomber original known for its silky, gardenia mix–enriched texture.

name: Saturn
instructions: Combine all ingredients in a shaker with ice. Shake well. Strain into a chilled tiki glass or large coupe. Garnish with a cherry and mint. Created by J. 'Popo' Galsini for the 1967 IBA World Cocktail Championship. Gin-based tiki — a rare and wonderful thing.

name: Fog Cutter
instructions: Combine all ingredients except sherry in a shaker with ice. Shake well. Strain over crushed ice into a tiki mug. Float the sweet sherry on top. Created by Trader Vic Bergeron in 1945. One of the few tiki drinks to feature three base spirits.

name: Japanese Highball
instructions: Chill a highball glass thoroughly. Add one large ice cube and stir 13 times to chill the glass. Pour in the whisky and stir 3 times clockwise. Top slowly with chilled carbonated water and stir once from bottom to top. Express a lemon peel and drop in. Technique is everything — carbonation must be preserved.

name: Wray & Ting
instructions: Fill a highball glass with ice. Add overproof rum. Top with Ting grapefruit soda and stir gently. Squeeze in a lime wedge and drop in. One of the simplest and most refreshing highballs — the Jamaican rum funk paired with tart grapefruit is elemental.

name: Fernet & Coke
instructions: Fill a highball glass with ice. Pour in Fernet-Branca. Top with cola and stir gently once or twice. Add a lime wedge. The national drink of Argentina — known locally as 'Fernando'. The bitterness of Fernet and the sweetness of cola is more than the sum of its parts.

name: Pimm's Cup
instructions: Fill a highball glass with ice. Pour in Pimm's No. 1. Top with lemonade (or ginger ale) and stir gently. Load with garnishes — the more the better. A cucumber ribbon is essential. The quintessential British summer drink, inseparable from Wimbledon and Henley.

name: Vermouth & Soda
instructions: Fill a wine glass or highball with ice. Pour in dry or white vermouth. Top with chilled sparkling water and stir gently once. Add garnish of choice. An aperitivo staple across Italy and Spain — refreshing, low-alcohol, and endlessly flexible.

name: Grasshopper
instructions: Combine all ingredients in a shaker with ice. Shake vigorously until very cold and frothy. Double-strain into a chilled coupe. Garnish with chocolate shavings or a small mint sprig. Created at Tujague's in New Orleans around 1919. Minty, creamy, and entirely retro.

name: Chartreuse Swizzle
instructions: Add all ingredients to a highball glass filled with crushed ice. Swizzle vigorously using a barspoon between palms until the glass frosts. Top with more crushed ice and garnish. Created by Marcovaldo Dionysos at Absinthe Brasserie, San Francisco. Green Chartreuse in its purest and most refreshing form.

name: Bloody Caesar
instructions: Rim a highball glass with celery salt. Fill with ice. Add vodka, Clamato juice, Worcestershire, hot sauce, and seasonings. Stir gently. Garnish generously — the Caesar is known for its elaborate garnishes. Invented by Walter Chell in Calgary, 1969. Canada's national cocktail.

name: Army and Navy
instructions: Combine gin, lemon juice, and orgeat in a shaker with ice. Add a dash of Angostura bitters if desired — it adds complexity and deepens the color. Shake well and double-strain into a chilled coupe. Express a lemon or grapefruit peel over the surface. A Prohibition-era gin sour made silky and nutty by the orgeat.

name: Mocha Martini
instructions: Combine all ingredients in a shaker with ice. Shake hard for 12 seconds to get a good foam. Double-strain into a chilled martini glass. Garnish with three espresso beans. A richer, chocolate-forward riff on the Espresso Martini — crème de cacao replaces the coffee liqueur. Chocolate bitters are an excellent addition.

name: Final Ward
instructions: Combine all ingredients in equal parts in a shaker with ice. Shake well and double-strain into a chilled coupe. Garnish with a lemon twist. The Final Ward is a Last Word riff by Phil Ward — rye replaces gin and lemon replaces lime, shifting the whole drink warmer and drier.

name: Midori Sour
instructions: Add all ingredients to a rocks glass filled with ice and stir, or shake with ice and strain over fresh ice. Adjust Midori to taste — more Midori gives a sweeter, more melon-forward drink. Top with a small dash of club soda or lemon-lime soda if desired. Garnish with a lemon wheel.

name: Smoke on the Boulevard
instructions: Combine all ingredients in a mixing glass with ice. Stir for 30 seconds until well chilled. Strain into a chilled coupe. Garnish with a brandied cherry. A stirred, spirit-forward riff that splits the difference between a Boulevardier and a Black Manhattan — the Aperol brightens, the Averna deepens Averna works well as the amaro component.

name: Left Hand
instructions: Combine all ingredients in a mixing glass with ice. Stir for 30 seconds until well chilled and properly diluted. Strain into a chilled coupe. Garnish with a brandied cherry. The Left Hand is a Manhattan/Negroni mashup by Sam Ross — the chocolate bitters tie the rye and Campari together beautifully.

name: Cranberry Sour
instructions: Blend defrosted cranberries with Cointreau, amaretto, orange juice, lime juice, and maple syrup until smooth. Strain through a fine sieve, pressing pulp firmly to extract all liquid. Chill thoroughly. To serve, fill small glasses or coupes with crushed ice and pour over the deep pink cocktail. Can be made in large batches ahead of time.

name: Spanish G&T
instructions: Fill a large wine glass or copa de balon with ice. Add gin and top slowly with tonic — Mediterranean tonic adds extra citrus character. Do not stir; let the tonic settle naturally. Load generously with aromatics: rosemary, mint, basil, juniper berries, peppercorns, citrus peels, and a long thin cucumber slice. Each garnish should be chosen to complement the gin's botanicals.

name: Cardinale
instructions: Combine gin, Campari, and dry vermouth in a mixing glass with ice. Stir for 30 seconds. Strain over a large ice cube into a rocks glass. Express a lemon peel over the surface and drop in or rest on the rim. The Cardinale is a Negroni with dry vermouth in place of sweet — drier, more bitter, and more aromatic.

name: Amaro Sour
instructions: Combine all ingredients in a shaker without ice. Dry shake for 20–30 seconds to emulsify the egg white fully. Add ice and shake hard for another 15 seconds. Strain into a rocks glass over a large ice cube. The egg white gives a silky, frothy texture; the bourbon adds backbone to balance the bitter amaro Averna is an excellent amaro choice here.

name: The Cigar
instructions: Combine sweet vermouth, Laphroaig, and Aperol in a mixing glass with ice. Stir for 30–40 seconds until well chilled. Strain into a chilled coupe. Express grapefruit oil over the surface by squeezing a wide strip of grapefruit peel — do not drop it in. Garnish with a scorched cinnamon stick if desired. Cocchi Vermouth di Torino is the ideal sweet vermouth here — its bitter orange notes complement the peated scotch beautifully. in a mixing glass with ice. Stir for 30–40 seconds until well chilled. Strain into a chilled coupe. Express grapefruit oil over the surface by squeezing a wide strip of grapefruit peel — do not drop it in. Garnish with a scorched cinnamon stick if desired. The peated scotch, bitter orange, and sweet vermouth create something that genuinely evokes a good cigar.

name: Smoke and Fire
instructions: Rim a chilled rocks glass with lime juice and sea salt. Combine all ingredients in a shaker with ice and shake until well chilled. Strain into the salt-rimmed glass over a large ice cube or small cubes. Garnish with a lime wedge and a dried chile. The equal-parts tequila and mezcal split gives smoke and brightness; Ancho Reyes Verde adds a fresh green heat.

name: Debbie Don't
instructions: Combine all ingredients in a shaker with ice. Shake well until chilled. Double-strain into a chilled coupe. Garnish with a lemon twist. The reposado tequila and Averna make for an earthy, bittersweet base, balanced by the bright lemon and rounded sweetness of maple syrup Averna is recommended for the amaro.

name: Eggnog (Alton Brown)
instructions: 1. Separate the eggs and reserve whites for another use. 2. Beat yolks with sugar and nutmeg until lightened in color and falling off the whisk in a solid ribbon. 3. Combine dairy, booze, and salt in a second vessel, then slowly beat into the egg mixture. 4. Transfer to glass jars and refrigerate for a minimum of 2 weeks — a month is better, two months better still. The aging mellows and melds everything. 5. Serve in mugs or cups topped with freshly grated nutmeg. Can also be drunk immediately if you can't wait. Alton Brown's definitive aged eggnog — the booze cures the eggs so no cooking required.

name: Martini
instructions:
Chill your glass first. Add gin, dry vermouth, and a fat dash of orange bitters to a mixing glass with ice. Stir well until cold and properly diluted, about 30 seconds. Strain into the chilled glass. Express the lemon oil over the surface. Garnish with the peel. 

Find St. George Terroir gin and Dolin dry vermouth. This is a relatively wet (2:1) martini. Many prefer much drier — try 4:1 or even 6:1 gin to vermouth depending on your taste.

name: French Connection
instructions: Build in a rocks glass over ice. Add cognac and amaretto. Stir briefly to combine. No garnish needed — the drink speaks for itself. A simple equal-parts digestif-style drink from the 1970s.

name: Rusty Nail
instructions: Build in a rocks glass over a large ice cube. Add Scotch, then Drambuie. Stir gently to combine without over-diluting. Garnish with a lemon twist. The Drambuie brings honey and heather sweetness to the Scotch; use a ratio anywhere from 2:1 to 4:1 depending on how sweet you like it.

name: Godfather
instructions: Build in a rocks glass over ice. Add Scotch and amaretto, stir briefly. A Godfather swaps Drambuie for amaretto — slightly nuttier and less sweet than a Rusty Nail. Use 2:1 Scotch to amaretto as a starting point and adjust to taste.

name: Godmother
instructions: Build in a rocks glass over ice. Add vodka and amaretto, stir briefly. The Godmother is the vodka counterpart to the Godfather — clean and neutral where the Godfather is smoky. Great for those who find Scotch too assertive.

name: John Collins
instructions: Add bourbon to a highball glass over ice. Add lemon juice and simple syrup. Top with soda water and stir gently. Garnish with a lemon slice and cherry. Essentially a Tom Collins made with whiskey instead of gin — bourbon or rye both work well.

name: Jack Rose
instructions: Combine applejack or calvados, lemon juice, and grenadine in a shaker with ice. Shake vigorously for 15 seconds. Strain into a chilled coupe. Garnish with a lemon twist. The grenadine gives a beautiful blush color and pomegranate sweetness that plays against the apple brandy. Use quality grenadine if possible.

name: Widow's Kiss
instructions: Combine calvados or apple brandy, Yellow Chartreuse, Benedictine, and Angostura bitters in a mixing glass with ice. Stir for 30 seconds. Strain into a chilled coupe. Garnish with a brandied cherry. An 1895 classic from George Kappeler — Benedictine and Chartreuse together create extraordinary botanical depth.

name: Brooklyn
instructions: Combine rye whiskey, dry vermouth, maraschino liqueur, and a dash of bitters in a mixing glass with ice. Stir for 30 seconds. Strain into a chilled coupe. Garnish with a maraschino cherry. The Brooklyn swaps sweet vermouth for dry and adds maraschino — drier and more complex than a Manhattan. Traditionally calls for Amer Picon; Torani Amer or a small amount of Campari work as substitutes.

name: Tipperary
instructions: Combine Irish whiskey, sweet vermouth, and Green Chartreuse in a mixing glass with ice. Stir for 30 seconds. Strain into a chilled coupe. Garnish with an orange twist. A pre-Prohibition classic — the Green Chartreuse adds a wildly herbal quality that transforms whiskey and vermouth into something far greater than the sum of its parts.

name: Gin Fizz
instructions: Combine gin, lemon juice, and simple syrup in a shaker with ice. Shake well for 15 seconds. Strain into a chilled highball glass without ice. Top with soda water. Garnish with a lemon wheel. Key difference from a Tom Collins: no ice in the glass, keeping it colder and fizzier longer. Add an egg white and shake dry first for a Silver Fizz.

name: Pink Gin
instructions: Add Angostura bitters to a chilled Nick and Nora glass and swirl to coat, discarding the excess. Add chilled gin and stir briefly. No ice in the glass. Garnish with a lemon twist. A Royal Navy classic — bitters were originally prescribed for seasickness and sailors added gin to make it palatable. Plymouth gin is traditional.

name: Negroni Bianco
instructions: Combine gin, dry vermouth, and Suze in a mixing glass with ice. Stir for 25 to 30 seconds. Strain into a rocks glass over a large ice cube. Garnish with a grapefruit twist. Substitutes Suze for Campari and dry vermouth for sweet — the result is bracingly bitter, floral, and far more delicate than a classic Negroni. Suze is traditional; Salers or other gentian bitters also work.

name: Cucumber Gimlet
instructions: Muddle 4 to 5 cucumber slices in a shaker. Add gin, lime juice, and simple syrup. Fill with ice and shake vigorously for 15 seconds. Double-strain through a fine mesh strainer into a chilled coupe. Garnish with a thin cucumber ribbon. Double-straining removes seeds and pulp. Hendricks gin is a natural pairing given its cucumber and rose notes.

name: Rum Old Fashioned
instructions: Add simple syrup and bitters to a rocks glass. Add dark or aged rum and stir to combine. Add a large ice cube and stir for 15 to 20 seconds until chilled. Express an orange peel over the glass and use as garnish. Aged Jamaican or Barbadian rum works beautifully — the molasses notes play a similar role to bourbons caramel.

name: Rum Sour
instructions: Dry shake rum, lemon juice, simple syrup, and egg white without ice for 10 to 12 seconds to emulsify. Add ice and shake hard for another 15 seconds. Strain into a rocks glass over ice. Garnish with a lemon wheel and cherry. Aged rum brings richness that pairs wonderfully with the egg white foam.

name: French Martini
instructions: Combine vodka, Chambord or other raspberry liqueur, and pineapple juice in a shaker with ice. Shake very vigorously for 15 seconds — the pineapple juice creates a natural foam. Strain into a chilled martini glass. Garnish with a pineapple wedge or fresh raspberry. The foam on top is a signature; it comes from the pineapple juice with no egg white needed.

name: Aperol Sour
instructions: Dry shake Aperol, lemon juice, simple syrup, and egg white without ice for 10 seconds. Add ice and shake hard for 15 seconds. Strain into a rocks glass over ice. Garnish with an orange slice and a few dots of Angostura bitters on the foam. Lower ABV than most sours and beautifully bitter-sweet.

name: Adonis
instructions: Combine dry sherry, sweet vermouth, and orange bitters in a mixing glass with ice. Stir for 30 seconds. Strain into a chilled coupe. Garnish with an orange twist expressed over the glass. The Bamboos sweeter sibling — fino or amontillado sherry with sweet vermouth creates a rich low-ABV aperitivo. Named after the 1884 Broadway musical.

name: M&M
instructions: Combine mezcal and Amaro Montenegro in a mixing glass with ice. Stir for 20–25 seconds until well chilled. Strain into a rocks glass over a large ice cube. Express an orange peel over the glass and use as garnish. Equal parts — a bartender's handshake. Short for Monte y Mezcal, created in 2015 by Marco Montefiori of Amaro Montenegro and popularised by Robert Krueger at Employee's Only in New York. So beloved among bartenders it earned the nickname 'Manager's Meeting'.

name: Espresso Martini (Better Version)
instructions: Shake all ingredients very vigorously with ice. Double-strain into a chilled martini glass. The foam comes from the espresso crema. Original recipe calls for 1/4 oz simple syrup, but I never use it with normal liquor, with Mr. Black it might be needed if you want it sweeter, but I like this ratio. Zacapa rum is nice in this.

name: Margarita (Future Marg)
instructions: muddle watermelon and chillies in shaker. Mix the rest. Salted rim with optional chili flakes or cayenne powder.

name: Enzoni (Fall)
instructions: Add grapes to a shaker and muddle gently. Add rye, Campari, lemon juice, and simple syrup. Shake with ice. Double-strain over fresh ice into a rocks glass. Garnish with a grape and orange slice.

name: De La Louisiane
instructions: Add all ingredients to a mixing glass with ice and stir until well-chilled. Strain into a chilled coupe glass. Garnish with a cherry.
